steps.title: Identify the visibility disparity
steps.content: The key signal is that common ravens can spot finch nests near road openings far more easily than nests in dense woodland.

steps.title: Apply visibility difference to predation
steps.content:
Finches nesting near the road will lose eggs to common ravens at a higher rate than finches nesting far away—a direct result of the differential spotting ability at each location. The other three either deny increased predation (A), propose illogical finch behavior (B), or address unrelated factors (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
